frameworks.name: fMRI neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: closed-loop brain imaging that lets participants modulate their own brain activity

frameworks.name: closed-loop brain imaging intervention
frameworks.one_line: real-time fMRI used as a therapeutic tool for mental disorders

frameworks.name: machine learning pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: decoders identify precise activity patterns to be targeted for feedback

frameworks.name: double-blind placebo-controlled trial methodology
frameworks.one_line: framework supporting rigorous causal evaluation of psychological interventions

core_question: Can contemporary real-time fMRI neurofeedback serve as a rigorous, targeted treatment for mental health disorders?
key_assumptions: mental disorders involve dysregulation of specific brain processes
brain activity patterns can be directly modulated via closed-loop feedback
neurofeedback designs admit double-blind placebo-controlled evaluation
manipulations: feedback signal targeting specific brain activity patterns
methodological guidelines applied to neurofeedback design
machine-learning-defined target patterns versus simpler regional signals
key_findings: neurofeedback historically failed to achieve mainstream clinical acceptance partly due to methodological issues
modern guidelines permit double-blind placebo-controlled neurofeedback trials
machine learning and statistics enable targeting of precise activity patterns for therapy
interpretations: rt-fMRI neurofeedback is a uniquely controllable, targeted psychological intervention
the approach is now positioned for legitimate clinical translation in mental health
limitations_noted: prior lack of mainstream acceptance due to methodological considerations
